text.title: React Hooks Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules: call-site discipline, dependency exhaustiveness, cleanup, derivation, measured memoization, custom-hook naming.

rule.statement: All hook calls (`useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef`, `useMemo`, `useCallback`, custom hooks) MUST appear at the top level of a function component or another hook. Never inside `if`, `for`, `switch`, `try`, after early `return`, or after `await`. Enforce via ESLint rule `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks: error`.
rule.rationale: React identifies hooks by call order. A conditional call shifts the order on subsequent renders, corrupting the linked-list of hook states and producing silent bugs ("Rendered fewer hooks than expected").

rule.statement: `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` MUST be set to `error`, never `warn`. Every variable referenced inside an effect / memo / callback closure must appear in its dependency array (or be wrapped in a ref).
rule.rationale: Stale closures over mutable values are the #1 source of "it works once then breaks" bugs. Warn-level lets the warning accumulate in noise; error blocks merge until the dep array is correct or the code is restructured (e.g. moved to event handler).

rule.statement: Any `useEffect` that opens a subscription, timer, listener, fetch, or websocket MUST return a cleanup function. Cleanup function must release the same resource the effect acquired (symmetric pair).
rule.rationale: React 18 Strict Mode runs effects twice in development to surface missing cleanup. Memory leaks and double-firing event handlers are the visible failure; in concurrent mode they also cause race conditions.

rule.statement: If a value can be computed from props or other state during render, compute it inline — do NOT mirror it into `useState` with a `useEffect` that re-syncs. Pattern `useState(derived) + useEffect(() => setState(derived), [deps])` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Sync-via-effect runs twice (initial render, then post-effect re-render with new value), produces a stale frame in between, and triples the bug surface. Direct derivation is one render, no stale window.

rule.statement: `useMemo` / `useCallback` are added ONLY when (a) the value is passed to a `React.memo`'d child or (b) the value is a dependency of another hook OR (c) the computation has been profiled and exceeds 1ms. Otherwise leave unmemoized — adding memo costs more (allocation + comparison) than it saves on cheap renders.
rule.rationale: React 19 compiler auto-memoizes; manual memoization on simple values now mostly adds noise. The remaining justified cases are referential-equality contracts (memo'd children, hook deps) and measurable hot paths.

rule.statement: Every custom hook function MUST be named `use*` (camelCase, lowercase `u`). Only functions named `use*` may call other hooks. Enforce via the same `rules-of-hooks` lint.
rule.rationale: Linter detects hook-call sites by name. A custom hook not prefixed `use` cannot be linted for hook rules and silently breaks rules-of-hooks coverage.
